summary_text: 1. `High` — Tone violations currently bypass the blocked workflow and become raw rejects. At [xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L804](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L804), tone failures return `None, {"reason": ...}`. They never become candidates, so they miss `blocked_reason_text`, `operator_action`, and blocked counters that are wired through [#L860](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L860), [#L884](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L884), [#L899](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L899), and [#L984](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L984). 2. `High` — No explicit tone-profile override path exists, so “polite mode” is impossible without weakening defaults globally. Tone gate is hardcoded in [#L395](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L395)-[#L410](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L410), and CLI has no `tone-profile` option in [#L944](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L944)-[#L958](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L958). 3. `Medium` — Regression coverage is too narrow to protect pipeline behavior. Current test only checks helper outputs at [test-xauto-tone-balance.sh#L19](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-xauto-tone-balance.sh#L19); it does not verify candidate lifecycle transitions (`blocked` vs `rejected`) in [xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L976](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L976)-[#L1043](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L1043). **Safest minimal insertion plan** 1. Convert tone failures into blocked candidates with operator guidance: - In [build_candidate at #L785](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L785), stop returning early at [#L804](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L804)-[#L806](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L806). - Build `candidate` first, then if eligible, set: - `candidate["review_eligibility"] = "blocked"` - `candidate["review_blockers"] = [tone_reason]` - In [main blocked branch at #L984](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L984), use the actual blocker (`candidate["review_blockers"][0]`) instead of hardcoding `"missing-non-x-primary-source"` at [#L987](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L987). - Extend reason maps at [#L860](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L860)-[#L881](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L881) for: - `tone-balance-assertive-overload` - `tone-balance-overpolite-overload` 2. Add explicit tone-profile override while keeping default middle register: - Add CLI/env config in parser block [#L944](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto_generate_drafts_from_registry.py#L944)-[#L961](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/i...
